entity.summary: Solana-based non-custodial decentralized exchange and shared central-limit-order-book protocol launched in August 2020. Serum became unsafe to rely on after the November 2022 FTX security incident because FTX retained the program upgrade authority. Solana ecosystem developers deployed the community-controlled OpenBook fork, and Project Serum stated on 2022-11-29 that the original Serum mainnet program had become defunct.
entity.notes: HEI uses death_reason hack because the terminal abandonment of the original program followed the FTX security incident and loss of trust in FTX-controlled upgrade authority. This does not assert a direct loss from the Serum order book; the decisive issue was that program control could no longer be treated as secure. OpenBook is recorded as the community fork and functional replacement, but successor_id remains null until OpenBook has its own canonical HEI entity.

events.title: Serum DEX went live on Solana
events.description: Serum launched as a non-custodial decentralized exchange and on-chain central-limit-order-book protocol on Solana. Project Serum later described the protocol as having gone live in August 2020, while contemporaneous launch reporting dated the live deployment to 2020-08-30.
events.notes: The exact day follows contemporaneous launch reporting; the project itself confirms the August 2020 launch month.

events.title: FTX security incident made Serum program control unsafe
events.description: After unauthorized activity affected FTX, Solana ecosystem developers concluded that Serum's FTX-controlled upgrade authority could no longer be trusted. Developers copied Serum v3 into a newly deployed, multisig-controlled program that became OpenBook, while major integrations moved away from the original Serum program.
events.notes: The event is classified as exploit because the terminal security response followed the FTX incident and loss of confidence in the Serum upgrade authority. No direct loss from the Serum order book is asserted.

events.title: Project Serum declared the original mainnet program defunct
events.description: Project Serum stated that the collapse of Alameda and FTX had rendered the original Serum mainnet program defunct. It pointed users and protocols toward the community-led OpenBook fork, noting that Serum liquidity and volume had fallen to near zero and that the old program carried security risk.
events.notes: Used as the terminal date because the project's own account explicitly described the original mainnet program as defunct.

evidence.title: Serum Portal is Live
evidence.notes: Primary retrospective source stating that Serum went live in August 2020 as a non-custodial DEX powered by Solana.

evidence.title: FTX's Serum goes live as DEXs end another record-breaking month for volume
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous report establishing the exact live-launch date.

evidence.title: Monthly Community Update — December 2022
evidence.notes: Official Solana ecosystem account of FTX retaining Serum code authority, the resulting integrity risk, and the rapid deployment of OpenBook as a replacement fork.

evidence.title: OpenBook resources and Serum community fork FAQ
evidence.notes: Primary successor-project source explaining that FTX controlled the Serum authority and that OpenBook redeployed the code under ecosystem multisig control.

evidence.title: Serum exchange rendered 'defunct' following the collapse of Alameda and FTX
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous report describing the upgrade-authority risk, ecosystem fork response, and migration away from the old Serum program.

evidence.title: Project Serum update declaring the mainnet program defunct
evidence.notes: Primary project statement that the Serum mainnet program had become defunct after the collapse of Alameda and FTX because FTX held the upgrade authority.

evidence.title: FTX-backed Serum protocol has ceased operations
evidence.notes: Contemporaneous report preserving the official Serum statements on defunct status, security risk, OpenBook adoption, and near-zero legacy liquidity.

evidence.title: FTX-backed DEX Serum calls itself 'defunct,' promotes community fork
evidence.notes: Independent contemporaneous confirmation that Project Serum called the original protocol defunct and directed users toward OpenBook.
